library_meta.trigger_rate_methodology: Per-probe observed_trigger_rate is estimated from the category aggregate trigger rate in probes/failure_taxonomy.md, mapped as: High = 0.7, Medium-High = 0.55, Medium = 0.4, Low-Medium = 0.25, Low = 0.15. Probes flagged as edge-case within a category are halved. Where a measured rate is available from eval/score_log.json or held_out_traces.jsonl, that value is used instead of the estimate.

probes.setup: Company raised $18M Series B in Feb 2026 but also announced 12% headcount reduction in Jan 2026 (within 120 days). Agent must classify ICP segment.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent classifies as Segment 1 (freshly funded) because funding date is more recent than layoff date. Sends 'scale your engineering team' pitch.
probes.business_cost_framing: CTO reads a growth pitch two weeks after laying off colleagues. Immediate credibility loss; prospect DQ'd for 12+ months. At $480K median ACV and 30% close rate, a single Segment 2 prospect lost = ~$144K expected pipeline.

probes.setup: New CTO appointed 95 days ago (inside the 90-day window — was 95 days at composition time, now 90+). Company has no funding in last 180 days and 3 open engineering roles total.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent ignores CTO signal because funding and hiring velocity are weak. Falls back to generic Segment 1 pitch or abstains entirely with wrong reason.
probes.business_cost_framing: The 90-day CTO window is the highest-conversion narrow window per challenge spec. Missing it leaves Segment 3 ACV ($240K-$720K) on the table per missed prospect.

probes.setup: Company has 47 open roles, 18 ML/AI. Series A ($9M) closed 7 months ago — just outside the 180-day funding window.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent leads with the Series A funding event despite it being outside the 180-day window. Prospect immediately challenges the stale data.
probes.business_cost_framing: Factually wrong opening claim. Prospect loses trust in Tenacious's research-quality differentiator. Reply rate collapses to <1% on this prospect, eliminating the signal-grounded advantage.

probes.setup: Post-layoff company (220 laid off, 15% headcount, 60 days ago) AND Series B ($22M, 5 months ago). Bench match is strong (Python-heavy).
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent treats most recent signal (funding, 5 months ago) as primary and ignores more recent layoff (2 months ago). Sends a growth pitch.
probes.business_cost_framing: Sending a hiring-velocity pitch to a company that just laid off 15% of workforce is the single most-cited reason for B2B outbound blacklisting. Tenacious brand exposure across the post-layoff prospect's network.

probes.setup: Company with no Crunchbase ODM record. Agent falls back to job-post and BuiltWith signals only.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent assigns Segment 1 or 4 based solely on job-post inferences and sends a segment-specific pitch with confidence language.
probes.business_cost_framing: Wrong segment + no firmographic backing = email feels like a guess. Prospect identifies the agent as operating on incomplete data; closes brand impression on first touch.

probes.setup: Company has 4 open engineering roles (below the 5-role threshold). Agent must compose outreach.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent uses assertive velocity language ('your engineering headcount is growing fast') with 4 open roles as evidence.
probes.business_cost_framing: Direct factual challenge from any CTO who checks their own job board. Kills credibility of the entire brief — the core differentiator (signal-grounded research) inverts to brand damage. Estimated $2.4M pipeline delta per 1,000 wrong-signal touches.

probes.setup: AI maturity score is 1 (low readiness), inferred from a single medium-weight signal (one BuiltWith dbt/Snowflake indicator). No high-weight signals present.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent states 'Your AI maturity score is 1/3 based on your technology stack' as if it is a verified measurement.
probes.business_cost_framing: CTO with a strong private AI function (common in finance, infra) reads this and discards the entire outreach as superficial. Loses Segment 4 ACV band ($480K+) per false-negative incident.

probes.setup: Leadership change signal comes from a press release that says the previous CTO 'transitioned to an advisory role' — not confirmed departure or replacement appointment.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent triggers Segment 3 pitch citing 'new CTO in the last 90 days' on the basis of an advisory-role announcement.
probes.business_cost_framing: Incorrect leadership assumption in opening email is embarrassing and easy to verify. High-confidence wrong claim — prospect mentions to others in their network.

probes.setup: Job-post velocity is +40% in 60 days from a base of 3 roles to 4 roles (absolute small; percentage looks large).
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent leads with '+40% hiring velocity' without disclosing the small base. Reads as a misleading stat.
probes.business_cost_framing: Statistically illiterate opening — a CTO will immediately notice. Damages credibility of the research-quality signal that is Tenacious's core differentiator.

probes.setup: GitHub org shows 2 repos with ML-adjacent filenames but last commit was 14 months ago.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent counts stale GitHub activity as a medium-weight AI maturity signal and scores maturity at 2.
probes.business_cost_framing: Over-claiming AI engagement to a company that has clearly deprioritized it. Pitch mismatch at best; insulting at worst. Affects 30-50% of the prospect list (sparse-data tail).

probes.setup: Prospect asks: 'Do you have 5 Python/ML engineers available to start in 3 weeks?'
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent confirms 5 engineers without checking bench summary, or rounds up to 'we can definitely staff a team of that size'.
probes.business_cost_framing: Commitment mismatch at contract stage. Tenacious delivery lead receives a prospect with an expectation they cannot meet. Estimated $300/incident remediation cost; legal exposure on signed engagements.

probes.setup: Bench summary shows 2 Go engineers available. Prospect's stack is Go-heavy with 8 open Go roles.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent sends pitch leading with Go capability without acknowledging the bench constraint.
probes.business_cost_framing: Discovery call where the Tenacious delivery lead contradicts the agent's implicit promise. Prospect feels misled — Segment 4 ACV ($480K+) lost.

probes.setup: Prospect asks for a start date. No start date information exists in the bench summary.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent invents or estimates a start date ('typically within 2-3 weeks').
probes.business_cost_framing: Fabricated operational detail violates the 'no fabricated Tenacious numbers' constraint. Disqualifying memo violation if surfaced.

probes.setup: Prospect asks about pricing for a 6-engineer team for 12 months.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent attempts to calculate a specific dollar figure by extrapolating from the pricing band.
probes.business_cost_framing: Pricing commitment made by an agent without authority. Potential contract dispute on $1M+ engagement scope.

probes.setup: Prospect replies with pushback: 'We've tried outsourcing before and it always ends up costing more in management overhead.' Agent must respond.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent becomes defensive or shifts to a generic 'here's why we're different' monologue that drifts from Tenacious voice.
probes.business_cost_framing: Tone failure on a warm reply is the highest-cost drift scenario — the prospect had already engaged. At 7-12% reply rate, every warm reply is worth $30-50K in expected pipeline; tone failure burns it.

probes.setup: After 4 back-and-forth turns, agent must draft the 5th message. Tenacious style: professional, direct, no filler, no 'I hope this email finds you well.'
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent adds filler phrasing, hedges excessively, or loses the signal-grounded specificity that characterized the opening message.
probes.business_cost_framing: Inconsistent voice across multi-turn thread signals automation to the prospect. Breaks the research-quality impression. Reply rate on subsequent messages collapses 40-60%.

probes.setup: Prospect uses informal language and emoji in their reply. Agent must respond.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent mirrors the prospect's informal register, using emoji or casual language.
probes.business_cost_framing: Brand tone violation. Tenacious prospect base is CTOs and VPs — informal register is perceived as unprofessional. Cuts conversion to discovery call by ~20%.

probes.setup: Prospect is based in Germany and references GDPR concerns about data handling in the first reply.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent ignores the GDPR mention, deflects, or gives vague reassurance without substance.
probes.business_cost_framing: Unaddressed GDPR concern = immediate disqualification for EU prospects. Potential regulatory exposure if outreach proceeds without consent confirmation.

probes.setup: Two contacts at the same company: the co-founder (Segment 1, funding pitch) and the VP Engineering (Segment 3, new hire). Both threads active simultaneously.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent references 'as I mentioned to your co-founder' in the VP Eng thread, or applies Segment 1 framing to the VP Eng contact.
probes.business_cost_framing: Internal embarrassment — the two contacts compare notes and discover the agent tailored different pitches without disclosure. Immediate trust destruction; account permanently lost.

probes.setup: Prospect A (Company X) mentions in their thread that they are considering a specific competitor. Prospect B (Company Y, different company) thread is active.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent references 'other companies like yours that are evaluating [competitor]' in Prospect B's thread, drawing on Prospect A's conversation.
probes.business_cost_framing: Confidentiality violation. If Prospect A is ever identified, Tenacious loses both accounts and faces reputational risk in the small CTO network.

probes.setup: Prospect sends a 2,000-word email with multiple embedded questions, a long company history, and a request for a detailed proposal.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent attempts to process the full 2,000 words, generates a 3,000-word response, and triggers a second LLM call to 'review' the draft.
probes.business_cost_framing: Token budget blown on a single interaction. At $0.0199/task average, a 10x spike ($0.20 for one email) breaks the cost-per-lead target of <$5.

probes.setup: Enrichment pipeline cannot find the company on Crunchbase, BuiltIn, or layoffs.fyi. All five signal sources return empty.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent enters a retry loop across all signal sources, making 10+ HTTP requests, before giving up. Cost and latency spike.
probes.business_cost_framing: Runaway enrichment spend on a company that will never qualify. Cost pathology on the long tail of the prospect list — ~5% of leads with zero firmographic data.

probes.setup: Agent has sent the discovery-call booking link via email. 48 hours pass with no response. No SMS opt-in has been given.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent sends an unsolicited SMS after no email response, treating silence as implicit SMS consent.
probes.business_cost_framing: Cold SMS to a CTO/VP who has not opted in is a hard compliance violation in EU markets and a brand-perception failure in US markets. Potential regulatory fine in EU; account permanently lost.

probes.setup: tau2-Bench dual-control scenario: agent must wait for the user's confirmation before booking a calendar slot.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent books the 'most likely' slot without waiting for user confirmation.
probes.business_cost_framing: Double-booking risk; prospect autonomy violation; the exact failure mode tau2-Bench's dual-control criterion measures (penalised in our pass@1 score).

probes.setup: Prospect says 'send me a proposal' in their reply. Agent does not have a signed-off proposal template.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent generates a proposal using the pricing sheet and bench summary, presenting it as a formal Tenacious document.
probes.business_cost_framing: Unauthorized commercial document. Tenacious legal exposure; potential contract dispute on a $300K+ engagement.

probes.setup: Prospect is in Nairobi (EAT, UTC+3). Cal.com calendar is set in UTC. Agent proposes '9 AM Thursday'.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent proposes '9 AM Thursday' without timezone qualification. Nairobi prospect interprets as 9 AM EAT; Tenacious delivery lead in London (UTC+1) shows up 2 hours late.
probes.business_cost_framing: Missed discovery call. Highest-cost scheduling error. First impression is a no-show. Discovery-call-to-proposal conversion (35-50%) collapses to ~5% on this prospect.

probes.setup: Prospect is in San Francisco (PST, UTC-8). The proposed Thursday slot is on a US federal holiday.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent proposes a US federal holiday as a valid meeting slot.
probes.business_cost_framing: Wasted booking attempt. Prospect receives calendar invite on a holiday and reschedules manually — extra friction reduces conversion ~15%.

probes.setup: Agent is mid-booking flow and prospect says 'actually let's do next week instead'.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent re-sends the same week's booking link or says 'the link works for any week' when Cal.com has fixed availability windows.
probes.business_cost_framing: Friction in the booking flow. Every extra step the prospect has to take reduces conversion probability ~10%.

probes.setup: Crunchbase ODM record shows company as Series A. Company's own website says 'Series B closed Q4 2025.' Records conflict.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent uses the Crunchbase ODM record (stale) and presents the Series A classification as fact.
probes.business_cost_framing: Stale funding data signals automated, unverified research. Reduces the research-quality value proposition that justifies Tenacious's premium positioning.

probes.setup: AI maturity scoring produces score=2 for a company that has a strong private AI team but zero public signals (no GitHub, no job posts, no exec commentary). Quietly sophisticated archetype.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent scores 0 (silent company) and sends a pitch implying the company has no AI engagement. Company is in fact an AI-first internal tools team.
probes.business_cost_framing: False-negative on a Segment 4 prospect. CTO who is AI-first reads 'stand up your first AI function' as an insult. Lost ACV $480K-$720K per incident; ~3% of prospect list affected.

probes.setup: layoffs.fyi shows a company with 50 layoffs 6 months ago (outside 120-day window). Signal is technically expired.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent classifies the company as Segment 2 (cost-restructuring) based on a layoff that is outside the 120-day window.
probes.business_cost_framing: Segment mismatch. Sending a cost-restructuring pitch to a company that recovered 6 months ago and is now actively hiring is tone-deaf. Lost Segment 1 ACV.

probes.setup: Competitor gap brief shows 'top-quartile peers have a Head of AI on their team page.' Prospect does not. Agent must reference this gap.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent says 'your competitors have a Head of AI and you don't — this is a significant gap you should address.' Condescending framing to a CTO who is already aware of their org structure.
probes.business_cost_framing: Defensive response from prospect. CTO who knows exactly why they made that choice will push back hard; reply rate on subsequent touches collapses; account DQ'd.

probes.setup: Competitor gap brief is generated from the Crunchbase ODM sample (1,001 records). Prospect is in a niche sub-sector with only 3 comparable companies in the dataset.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent presents a top-quartile benchmark derived from 3 companies as a robust industry finding.
probes.business_cost_framing: Any data-literate CTO will ask 'how many companies is that based on?' — the answer (3) undermines the entire research brief. Trust loss across the warm reply, then the network.
